content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 .

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441 322-6444

July 7, 1983

Mr. Dexter Ahlgren
20320 Highway 9
Boulder Creek, CA 95006

Re: Advice No. A-83-153

Dear Mr. Ahlgren:

Ms. Milman asked me to respond to your request for a written
confirmation of her telephone advice to you. The facts, as
presented in your letter, are that Santa Cruz County owns a road
known as the Saratoga Toll Road. The lower 0.93 miles of the
road is paved and maintained by the County and the remaining
eight miles is unimproved and badly deteriorated. Approximately
one mile of the unimproved road passes through watershed land
owned by the San Lorenzo Valley Water District. You are the
President of the Board of Directors of this District. Recently,
the State Parks Department asked the County to turn over its
control of the unimproved road to the Parks Department for
maintenance, control, operation and development. The Water
District is concerned that there will be inadequate environ-
mental protections and insufficient consideration of the area's
watershed function.

Your question is whether you must disqualify yourself from
any involvement in matters concerning the Saratoga Toll Road
because you own 16 acres of land fronting on the 0.93 miles of
road which is maintained.

Government Code Section 87100 1/ provides that no public
official shall make, or participate in the making of, a
governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know
he has a financial interest. An official has a "financial
interest" within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is

1/ Hereinafter all statutory references made are to the
Government Code unless otherwise indicated.

Mr. Dexter Ahlgren
July 7, 1983
Page Two


reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect on:

* * *

(b) Any real property in which the public official has a direct or indirect interest worth more than $1,000.

Thus, as a member of the Water District Board, you are required to disqualify yourself from making and participating in a decision concerning the unimproved portion of the Toll Road if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect2/ on your 16 acre parcel.

You stated on the telephone and in your letter that you can not foresee that any of the Toll Road decisions will have a material financial effect on your real property. Based on this representation, it is not necessary for you to disqualify yourself from these decisions. This advice is based solely on the facts that you have presented, as we have not made any independent factual determinations.

Thank you for contacting us on this matter. If I can be of any help to you in the future, please feel free to contact me at 916/322-5901.

Very truly yours,

Janis Shank McLean
Janis Shank McLean
Counsel, Legal Division

JSM:km

_______________________
2/ The phrase "material financial effect" is explained in 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18702 (copy enclosed).

Ahlgren Vineyard
BONDED WINERY #3764

20320 HIGHWAY 9 • BOULDER CREEK, SANTA CRUZ COUNTY, CALIFORNIA • (408) 338-6071

June 20, 1983

Ms. Barbara Milman
Chief of Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Ms. Milman;

I am writing to confirm my telephone discussion with you on Thursday,
June 16, 1983, regarding a question of conflict of interest.

I am an elected member of the Board of Directors of the San Lorenzo Valley
Water District, Santa Cruz County, California and am currently serving as
President of the Board.

An issue of long standing public controversy involving matters of environmental impact is that of the Saratoga Toll Road Trail. The Saratoga Toll
Road was developed in the middle 1800's as a toll road. After less than a
decade of use it was abandoned. In 1890 the County of Santa Cruz acquired
the rights held in the Toll Road by the original owners. It is alleged
that the County's interest is that of a prescriptive easement. The lower
0.93 miles of the Saratoga Toll Road exists today as a paved County maintained road. The remaining approximately eight miles of the Saratoga
Toll Road have never been maintained or improved by the County and has
badly deteriorated in many places to a little used trail. Approximately
one mile of this trail passes through privately owned property, approximately five miles passes through land purchased by Sempervirons for the
State Parks Department and approximately one mile passes through watershed lands owned by the San Lorenzo Valley Water District.

WINES FROM THE WINEMAKERS

Page Two, Letter to Ms. Barbara Milman, June 20, 1983


My wife and I own a sixteen acre parcel of land which has frontage along
the 0.93 miles of County maintained Toll Road. The parcel is in agricul-
tural use.

The State Parks Department has requested the County to turn over control
of the Toll Road Trail, minus the 0.93 miles of County maintained road,
to the Parks Department for maintenance, control, operation and develop-
ment. The San Lorenzo Valley Water District is concerned that there is
little or no environmental protection and no consideration of the fact
that the entire area containing the Toll Road Trail is on a water
supply watershed for a number of downstream communities within our Dis-
trict.

An opinion has been put forth holding that I am in conflict of interest
involving Water District matters relating to the Toll Road Trail because
of my ownership of a parcel of land fronting the County maintained portion
of the Saratoga Toll Road.

I have thought long and hard about this possibility, but cannot imagine
any way that I might be financially affected by the status of the Toll
Road Trail, either positively or negatively.

In my discussion with you on June 16, 1983, we reviewed the situation
together as recapped above. You mentioned "material financial effect on my
property" and the basis for evaluating the question of conflict. Neither
you nor I could come up with an idea that might suggest any financial effect
on my property. Since I cannot imagine any way that the status of the Toll
Road Trail could impact on me, my land or my family in any way that could
result in a conflict of interest, I must conclude that none exists.

Page three, Letter to Ms. Barbara Milman, June 20, 1983


I have your verbal opinion, but would very much appreciate your response
in writing. There is some urgency to this since the matter of the Saratoga
Toll Road Trail is currently under consideration by my Board and is
developing rapidly.

Very truly yours,

Dexter D. Ahlgren

cc: Board of Directors, S.L.V.W.D.

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660              322-5901              322-5441              322-6444

June 27, 1983


Dexter D. Ahlgren
Ahlgren Vineyard
20320 Highway 9
Boulder Creek,
    Santa Cruz County, CA 95006
                                    Re: A-83-153

Dear Mr. Ahlgren:

    Your letter requesting advice under the Political
Reform Act has been referred to me. If you have any
questions about your advice request, please contact me
directly at (916) 322-5901.

    We try to answer all advice requests promptly.
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21
working days.

                            Very truly yours,


                            [illegible]
                            Barbara A. Milman
                            Chief
                            Legal Division

BAM:km
Enclosure

sections.question: Whether you must disqualify yourself from any involvement in matters concerning the Saratoga Toll Road because you own 16 acres of land fronting on the 0.93 miles of road which is maintained.
sections.conclusion: Based on Government Code Section 87100, as a member of the Water District Board, you are required to disqualify yourself from making and participating in a decision concerning the unimproved portion of the Toll Road if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect on your 16 acre parcel. You stated that you cannot foresee that any of the Toll Road decisions will have a material financial effect on your real property. Based on this representation, it is not necessary for you to disqualify yourself from these decisions.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester, as President of the San Lorenzo Valley Water District Board, asks whether he must disqualify himself from decisions regarding the Saratoga Toll Road given his ownership of 16 acres fronting the maintained portion of that road.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that disqualification is required under Government Code Section 87100 only if decisions would have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on the requester's 16-acre parcel. Since the requester represented he foresees no such financial effect, disqualification is not necessary.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both incoming request letter (June 20, 1983) and FPPC response letter (July 7, 1983). The FPPC response is the primary advice letter. Incoming request letter text appears incomplete/damaged in latter portions. Confidence slightly reduced due to formatting issues in source material.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether you must disqualify yourself from any involvement in matters concerning the Saratoga Toll Road because you own 16 acres of land fronting on the 0.93 miles of road which is maintained.

CONCLUSION: Based on Government Code Section 87100, as a member of the Water District Board, you are required to disqualify yourself from making and participating in a decision concerning the unimproved portion of the Toll Road if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect on your 16 acre parcel. You stated that you cannot foresee that any of the Toll Road decisions will have a material financial effect on your real property. Based on this representation, it is not necessary for you to disqualify yourself from these decisions.

embedding.summary: FPPC Advice No. A-83-153 addresses whether a Water District Board President with ownership of real property abutting the Saratoga Toll Road must disqualify himself from decisions regarding that road. The FPPC concludes disqualification is not required based on the requester's representation that no decisions will have a material financial effect on his property.
